hero.availability: Building multichain products and AI agents
hero.name: raghav sharma

aboutParagraphs: I build blockchain products across SVM and EVM stacks, from protocol logic and chain-aware backend systems to polished product surfaces that users can actually ship with.
I also like building products that utilize AI and blockchain to increase agentic economy, deeply researching agentic economy these days

bullets: Shipped a learning product used by 2,200+ developers with AI-assisted Solana and Rust support.
Built the content, RAG, sandbox, and production app stack end to end across product, infra, and data.
Reached 1,100+ completed exercises in production with zero critical learning-flow bugs.
Grew to 50+ GitHub stars and ecosystem recognition through focused developer education.
Built structural design modules across React, Django, and REST APIs for engineering users.
Implemented steel-design workflows with extensive validation logic based on IS 800:2007.
Resolved CAD-generation and session-state issues across multiple production modules.
Reduced debugging time materially through better logging, fault isolation, and API cleanup.
Built session-based persistence for a chat product handling 500+ daily conversations.
Created a feedback pipeline that captured 1,200+ ratings and improved model quality.
Fixed bugs affecting 5,000+ users and improved user satisfaction across the product.
Cut image delivery latency by moving media distribution onto CDN infrastructure.

projects.name: LearnSol
projects.shortDescription: Open-source learning platform for Solana developers with lessons, exercises, and embedded AI help.
projects.desc: Free, open-source Solana learning platform with project-based lessons, interactive exercises, gamified challenges, and built-in AI help.
projects.meta: 2K+ users · 50+ GitHub stars
projects.summary: Developer education product built from curriculum and app UX down to eval pipelines and a live code sandbox.
projects.overview: LearnSol packages structured lessons, runnable examples, AI support, and progressive exercises into a single developer learning product for Solana builders.
projects.problem: Solana education is often fragmented across docs, repos, and one-off tutorials, which slows down developers who need applied guidance and fast feedback.
projects.solution: I built a guided learning environment that combines curriculum, interactive exercises, AI-assisted explanation, and a Rust/Anchor execution workflow in one place.
projects.role: Founder, product engineer, and platform architect.
features: Structured lesson progression with project-based learning
AI assistant tuned for Solana, Rust, and Anchor help
Live coding and execution workflows for hands-on practice
architecture: Next.js frontend with server-side product flows
Retrieval pipeline over Solana and Rust documentation using pgvector
Sandboxed code execution path for practical exercises
Analytics and progression tracking to monitor learner outcomes
challenges: Keeping AI responses precise enough for technical education
Designing exercise flows that feel practical rather than toy examples
Balancing curriculum authoring with product and infra work
outcomes: 2,200+ users
1,100+ exercises completed
Solana Foundation grant recipient
Featured in Colosseum newsletter

projects.name: CipherPay
projects.shortDescription: Private payroll and payout workspace for crypto teams running single or batch disbursements on Solana.
projects.desc: Private payroll and payout workspace for Solana with single-pay and batch payroll flows, durable payout-run state, and a shielded private payout rail.
projects.meta: Private payouts · Batch payroll · ZK rail
projects.summary: Operations-heavy payout product combining UX, persistence, payroll orchestration, and privacy-focused rails.
projects.overview: CipherPay is a payroll and treasury workflow product for teams that need to run crypto payouts without exposing every compensation flow in a brittle manual process.
projects.problem: Crypto-native teams often stitch together wallets, spreadsheets, and one-off scripts to manage payouts, with weak privacy and little operational reliability.
projects.solution: I built a single workspace for one-off and batch payouts with durable payout state, auditability, and a private settlement path for sensitive disbursements.
projects.role: Product engineer across app architecture, backend workflows, and on-chain integration.
features: Shielded payout rail research and integration work
architecture: Next.js product layer with authenticated operator workflows
Postgres-backed payout state and job orchestration
Anchor and Solana integrations for transaction flows
Video demo support for hackathon and external review
challenges: Designing payout UX that remains fast under operational complexity
Persisting batch-run state safely across retries and failures
Bridging normal payroll expectations with crypto settlement constraints
outcomes: Hackathon-ready payout product
Private payouts and batch payroll in one surface
Case study for combining product UX with on-chain rails

projects.name: AvaLaunch
projects.shortDescription: AI agent that turns plain-English intent into a deterministic Avalanche L1 launch and management workflow.
projects.desc: AI agent for end-to-end L1 launch and management, turning plain English into a working Avalanche network with a deterministic launch plan.
projects.meta: AI agent · L1 deployment · Avalanche
projects.summary: Multichain positioning anchor: productized infrastructure for launching Avalanche L1s through an AI workflow.
projects.overview: AvaLaunch is an AI-assisted control surface for bootstrapping and managing Avalanche L1 networks through guided inputs and deterministic execution planning.
projects.problem: Launching an Avalanche L1 usually requires fragmented docs, careful infra sequencing, and operator experience that most product teams do not have in-house.
projects.solution: I translated launch intent into a structured execution plan, then built the product and backend flow needed to guide builders from idea to configured network.
projects.role: End-to-end builder across product UX, orchestration logic, and infrastructure-facing backend work.
architecture: TypeScript orchestration layer for action planning
challenges: Making a complex infra flow understandable for product teams
Reducing ambiguity in AI-assisted launch planning
Mapping chain-specific launch requirements into reliable product steps
outcomes: Production-style Avalanche workflow demo
Clear proof of EVM-side product engineering depth
Reusable model for chain-specific operator tooling

projects.name: AgentPay
projects.shortDescription: Agent-payment control surface that lets AI agents buy paid APIs with guardrails and onchain proof on Avalanche Fuji.
projects.desc: Agent-payment workspace for AI agents to buy paid APIs through x402, enforce policy-controlled spend, and write reputation proof on Avalanche Fuji.
projects.meta: Paid APIs · Spend guardrails · Fuji proof
projects.summary: Avalanche-side agent commerce product where policy checks, x402 payments, and reputation writes turn API spend into a controllable workflow.
projects.overview: AgentPay gives AI agents a way to discover paid APIs, request payment safely, execute paid tool calls, and leave a verifiable onchain trust trail on Avalanche Fuji.
projects.problem: Agents need access to live and specialized APIs, but direct wallet access without policy limits creates obvious overspend and trust problems.
projects.solution: I built a workflow where the model chooses tools, deterministic policy checks gate spend, x402 settles payment, and successful calls write validation and reputation proof onchain.
projects.role: Product engineer across frontend, agent workflow, payment integration, and smart-contract-backed trust primitives.
features: Model-driven paid API selection with allowance policy checks
Avalanche Fuji reputation and validation proof writes
Operator-visible receipts, rejected providers, and spend trace
architecture: Next.js app with agent console, registry views, and API routes
OpenAI-powered agent loop for paid tool selection
x402 payment integration for API unlocking
Solidity registries on Avalanche Fuji for identity, reputation, and validation
challenges: Keeping agent autonomy while preventing uncontrolled spend
Making paid API calls observable and explainable to operators
Bridging offchain inference and onchain trust proof cleanly
outcomes: Working Avalanche Fuji prototype
Paid API agent loop with policy-gated spend
Onchain receipts and reputation proof tied to successful calls

projects.name: Buy Me Some Tokens
projects.shortDescription: Base-native USDC tipping and identity layer for AI agents with onchain profiles, agent wallets, and direct tips.
projects.desc: Buy Me Some Tokens is a Base-native identity and USDC tipping product for AI agents. Agents register wallet-backed profiles onchain, profile and message content is anchored with IPFS-backed keccak256 hashes, and humans or agents can send canonical USDC tips through a dedicated tip jar contract.
projects.meta: Agent tipping · Base · USDC · IPFS
projects.summary: Base-native agent identity and USDC tipping product that treats agents as persistent economic actors with wallet-backed profiles and operator-linked dashboards.
projects.overview: Buy Me Some Tokens gives AI agents durable public identities, wallet ownership, direct USDC tipping, and human operator dashboards. The product uses Base for registration and canonical tip events, PostgreSQL for app projections, and IPFS-backed content hashes for profiles and messages.
projects.problem: AI agents are usually treated like temporary chat sessions rather than persistent economic actors that can own identity, receive value, and build public reputation.
projects.solution: I built a product where agents onboard with Base-compatible wallets, register onchain, publish IPFS-backed profiles, receive direct USDC tips, expose public tip feeds, and give operators durable dashboard access without taking custody of agent funds.
projects.role: Full-stack blockchain product engineer across frontend flows, API design, PostgreSQL projections, IPFS content anchoring, and Solidity contracts for agent registration and USDC tipping.
features: Agent registration and wallet-backed profile claims on Base
IPFS-backed agent profiles and public tip message hashes
Public agent directory ranked by tip activity
Dashboard claim-link flow for human operators
Authenticated agent APIs for discovery, tips, thanks, and prepared transactions
architecture: Next.js 16 app with UI, API routes, and hosted skill endpoints
PostgreSQL projection layer for feeds, links, tokens, dashboard state, and spending policies
IPFS storage adapter with local keccak256 content hashes
Solidity AgentRegistry and USDCTipJar contracts on Base
Indexer that syncs USDCTipJar events by chain, contract, and event name
challenges: Separating agent wallet ownership from operator dashboard access safely
Keeping canonical tips onchain while storing rich profile/message content offchain
Designing an onboarding flow that works for both humans and agent runtimes
Moving from native-token tips to USDC approval and transfer semantics
outcomes: Base-native USDC tipping and identity prototype
Clear agent-wallet case study with onchain registration and public reputation
Production-shaped architecture for agent profiles, dashboards, and indexed tip feeds

projects.name: Pigeon
projects.shortDescription: Privacy-focused wallet superapp with encrypted chat and on-chain message history for Solana users.
projects.desc: Privacy-focused Solana wallet superapp with encrypted wallet-to-wallet chat live on devnet, on-chain message history, and private transfer/swap research routes in progress.
projects.meta: Encrypted chat · Privacy · Live on Devnet
projects.summary: Wallet-native communication product exploring privacy and identity primitives on Solana.
projects.overview: Pigeon explores what a crypto wallet becomes when messaging, social context, and privacy-preserving transfers live inside the same user surface.
projects.problem: Wallets still feel transactional and contextless, while private communication and asset actions remain split across unrelated tools.
projects.solution: I built a wallet-oriented product with encrypted chat, wallet-linked identity, and research paths for private transfers and swaps.
projects.role: Product engineer for the app experience and Solana integration flow.
architecture: Anchor program integration on Solana devnet
challenges: Balancing privacy ambitions with hackathon delivery scope
Keeping user flows understandable in a wallet-native environment
Designing around on-chain history and encrypted communication tradeoffs
outcomes: Working devnet prototype
Encrypted messaging demonstrated in product form
Privacy-focused wallet direction validated through hackathon build

projects.name: CPMM
projects.shortDescription: Constant-product market maker on Solana with PDA-controlled vaults for swaps and liquidity.
projects.desc: On-chain constant product market maker on Solana. x·y=k for token swaps and liquidity provision. PDA-controlled token vaults. Deposit, swap, withdraw — atomic.
projects.meta: On-chain AMM · SPL tokens · PDA vaults
projects.summary: Protocol-level Solana AMM implementation focused on core swap and liquidity mechanics.
projects.overview: CPMM is a direct implementation of a constant-product AMM with atomic swap and liquidity flows on Solana.
projects.problem: AMM mechanics are simple conceptually but require careful token-vault, PDA, and instruction design to be safe and composable on-chain.
projects.solution: I implemented the core x*y=k protocol path with explicit vault ownership, liquidity operations, and atomic execution guarantees.
projects.role: Protocol engineer implementing the program logic and testing approach.
features: Token swaps against a constant-product curve
architecture: PDA authority model for vault ownership
challenges: Maintaining pricing invariants during swap execution
Designing safe token-vault authority patterns
Keeping protocol flows minimal but testable
outcomes: Complete AMM proof of work
Clear demonstration of DeFi protocol fundamentals
Strong supporting artifact for protocol engineering depth

projects.name: Escrow
projects.shortDescription: Trustless escrow program with PDA-controlled SPL vaults and atomic settlement paths.
projects.desc: Trustless on-chain escrow in Anchor/Rust. Deposit, refund, take — secured via PDA-controlled SPL token vaults. Atomic settlement, no off-chain intermediaries.
projects.meta: Trustless · PDA vaults · Atomic settlement
projects.summary: Minimal but concrete Solana escrow implementation focused on custody and settlement correctness.
projects.overview: Escrow implements a trustless deposit, refund, and take flow using PDA-managed custody on Solana.
projects.problem: Escrow is a classic primitive, but secure vault authority and clean settlement semantics still need careful program design.
projects.solution: I used PDA-controlled token custody and explicit instruction paths to make settlement atomic without relying on off-chain operators.
projects.role: Program author and tester.
challenges: Ensuring safe authority transfer semantics
Keeping the protocol minimal without weakening correctness
Mapping a familiar financial primitive into Solana account patterns
outcomes: Clean escrow proof of work
Foundational protocol example reusable for larger systems

projects.name: Flash Loan
projects.shortDescription: Flash loan protocol enforcing same-transaction repayment through instruction introspection.
projects.desc: Flash loan protocol in Anchor that lends from a PDA-controlled vault, enforces same-transaction repayment through instructions-sysvar introspection, and charges a fixed fee on atomic repay.
projects.meta: Atomic repay checks · PDA vault authority · LiteSVM tests
projects.summary: Protocol implementation focused on instruction-sysvar validation and atomic loan repayment guarantees.
projects.overview: This flash-loan program lends from a controlled vault and validates same-transaction repayment before completion.
projects.problem: Flash loans are only safe if repayment conditions are enforced atomically and instruction flow cannot be bypassed.
projects.solution: I used Solana instruction introspection and PDA custody to enforce repay-in-transaction guarantees with a fixed fee model.
projects.role: Protocol engineer building and testing the program.
challenges: Using sysvar introspection safely
Avoiding false assumptions about transaction ordering
Testing atomic protocol guarantees rigorously
outcomes: DeFi primitive implemented with explicit safety checks
Good evidence of low-level Solana protocol familiarity

projects.name: Pinocchio Vault
projects.shortDescription: Single-asset tokenized vault in Pinocchio with share minting, pause controls, and admin safeguards.
projects.desc: Single-asset tokenized vault in Pinocchio. Deposits SPL tokens into PDA custody, mints proportional vault shares, burns shares on withdraw, and enforces pause plus two-step admin controls.
projects.meta: Tokenized vault · PDA custody · LiteSVM-tested SBF
projects.summary: Vault system built outside the usual Anchor path to show lower-level Solana program fluency.
projects.overview: Pinocchio Vault implements a single-asset tokenized vault with minted share accounting and admin controls.
projects.problem: Tokenized vaults need clear proportional accounting, custody design, and administrative safety rails without overcomplicating the model.
projects.solution: I implemented custody, share minting, withdrawal, pause control, and two-step admin operations in a more bare-metal Solana framework.
projects.role: Program engineer using Pinocchio instead of Anchor.
architecture: SPL token custody and share mint logic
challenges: Managing correctness with less framework abstraction
Handling share accounting safely
Designing admin controls without creating operational confusion
outcomes: Clear proof of lower-level Solana program capability
Vault primitive usable as a base for broader protocol work

projects.name: MPMC Queue
projects.shortDescription: Bounded Rust multi-producer multi-consumer queue focused on correctness, throughput, and clean concurrency.
projects.desc: Bounded multi-producer multi-consumer queue in Rust. Mutex/Condvar, blocking + non-blocking modes. 72.3M ops/sec. 13 integration tests. Zero unsafe code.
projects.meta: 72.3M ops/sec · 13 tests · 0 unsafe
projects.summary: Systems-level Rust artifact demonstrating concurrency and performance fundamentals beyond blockchain-specific code.
projects.overview: This project implements a bounded MPMC queue with both blocking and non-blocking modes using safe Rust concurrency primitives.
projects.problem: Concurrency-heavy components require strong correctness guarantees under pressure, especially when avoiding unsafe shortcuts.
projects.solution: I built a high-throughput queue around Mutex and Condvar coordination, then benchmarked and tested it thoroughly.
projects.role: Rust systems engineer.
features: Safe Rust only, no unsafe code
challenges: Balancing throughput with clean coordination semantics
Avoiding deadlocks and starvation conditions
Preserving ergonomic API behavior
outcomes: 72.3M ops/sec benchmark
13 integration tests
Strong supporting proof of systems depth

projects.name: solana-txn-tui
projects.shortDescription: Published Rust terminal tool for exploring Solana transactions, accounts, fees, and token movements.
projects.desc: Published Rust crate for exploring Solana transactions and accounts in the terminal. cargo install, Homebrew, or curl. Fee breakdown, instruction details, token transfers. Mainnet / Devnet / Testnet.
projects.meta: crates.io · Homebrew · Windows binary · 4 releases
projects.summary: Developer tooling project turning raw RPC data into a usable terminal-native inspection flow.
projects.overview: solana-txn-tui is a published CLI and terminal UI for inspecting Solana transactions and account-level detail without leaving the terminal.
projects.problem: Raw RPC responses and explorer interfaces are often too noisy or too slow when developers need to inspect transaction behavior quickly.
projects.solution: I built a TUI that surfaces fees, instructions, token transfers, and account detail in a compact developer-focused workflow.
projects.role: Tooling engineer and crate maintainer.
features: Installable via cargo, Homebrew, and direct binary workflows
challenges: Presenting dense blockchain data clearly inside a terminal surface
Supporting multiple install paths
Keeping the tool fast for everyday developer usage
outcomes: Published crate and release pipeline
Practical developer utility for Solana debugging and inspection

projects.name: Observer
projects.shortDescription: Crash-safe Solana observability indexer with WebSocket slot updates, block fetches, and Postgres persistence.
projects.desc: Self-hosted Solana observability indexer in Rust. Uses WebSocket slot updates plus RPC block fetches, Postgres upserts, and a persisted cursor for crash-safe resume. Built for near real-time network health and protocol risk monitoring.
projects.meta: Crash-safe resume · Idempotent upserts · Protocol risk monitoring
projects.summary: Data infrastructure project for monitoring Solana network and protocol activity with durable ingestion semantics.
projects.overview: Observer ingests Solana chain activity through a crash-safe indexing flow designed for near-real-time monitoring and analysis.
projects.problem: Network and protocol monitoring needs durable ingestion, resumability, and enough structure to support downstream risk analysis.
projects.solution: I paired WebSocket slot updates with RPC block fetches, then wrote state into Postgres using idempotent persistence and a saved cursor.
projects.role: Infra engineer for ingestion, storage, and monitoring-oriented architecture.
challenges: Maintaining resumability without duplicate corruption
Bridging live feeds and historical fetches reliably
Keeping latency low enough for monitoring use cases
outcomes: Operationally credible observability indexer
Strong evidence of backend and data pipeline depth

projects.name: Omniscanner
projects.shortDescription: Borrower-risk scanner with local scoring, stress tracking, alerts, and a compact operator dashboard.
projects.desc: Rust borrower-risk scanner for Omnipair with local scoring, pool stress tracking, liquidation alerts, PostgreSQL persistence, and a small operator dashboard served from the app.
projects.meta: Borrower risk alerts · Axum API · Built-in dashboard
projects.summary: Risk monitoring product blending chain data, scoring logic, and a small operator-facing UI.
projects.overview: Omniscanner tracks borrower positions, pool stress, and liquidation risk through a backend scanner and lightweight dashboard.
projects.problem: DeFi risk monitoring needs more than raw chain ingestion; it needs local scoring and actionable operator views.
projects.solution: I built a scanner that computes borrower risk locally, persists state, raises alerts, and exposes an internal dashboard surface.
projects.role: Builder across backend scanner, scoring logic, persistence, and dashboard delivery.
challenges: Translating market state into actionable local risk scores
Keeping operator UX lightweight but useful
Persisting monitoring data cleanly for alerts and history
outcomes: End-to-end risk monitoring artifact
Good example of productized chain-data infrastructure

projects.name: Rayscan
projects.shortDescription: Search-first explorer with live transaction visibility, token pages, and indexed Solana backend data.
projects.desc: Search-first Solana explorer with live transaction visibility, token pages, network stats, and an indexed backend built with Fastify, WebSockets, and Postgres.
projects.meta: Explorer UI · Live tx feed · Indexed chain data
projects.summary: Explorer product centered on indexed data, live feeds, and search-oriented UX.
projects.overview: Rayscan is a search-first blockchain explorer built to make transaction, token, and network information easier to browse and monitor.
projects.problem: Generic explorers often overload users with information while still making important views harder to reach.
projects.solution: I built a cleaner search-driven explorer backed by indexed data, live transaction feeds, and focused token/network surfaces.
projects.role: Full-stack builder across frontend, backend, and chain-data indexing.
challenges: Structuring explorer data for fast retrieval
Designing search UX that feels direct
Keeping live transaction surfaces responsive
outcomes: Complete explorer proof of work
Strong full-stack chain-data product example

projects.name: PixelMart
projects.shortDescription: NFT marketplace with listings, buys, wallet flows, and Anchor-backed marketplace logic.
projects.desc: NFT marketplace on Solana with a Next.js frontend and an Anchor marketplace program for listings, buys, marketplace state, and wallet-signed flows.
projects.meta: NFT marketplace · Next.js + Anchor · Live demo
projects.summary: Marketplace build connecting consumer-facing UI with Solana NFT transaction logic.
projects.overview: PixelMart is an NFT marketplace with listing, buying, and state-management flows spanning frontend UX and on-chain program work.
projects.problem: NFT products require coherent wallet flows and marketplace logic, but many implementations feel fragmented or prototype-grade.
projects.solution: I built both the product layer and the marketplace program to support listings, purchases, and wallet-signed interactions end to end.
projects.role: Full-stack + on-chain engineer.
challenges: Keeping marketplace UX understandable
Mapping user actions cleanly into on-chain flows
Balancing demo readiness with protocol correctness
outcomes: End-to-end NFT marketplace proof of work
Clear example of consumer-facing blockchain product engineering

projects.name: Loopscale Borrow Shopper
projects.shortDescription: Consumer-facing borrow-routing app for finding and signing fixed-rate loan routes.
projects.desc: Consumer-facing Next.js app for finding fixed-rate borrow routes on Loopscale, reviewing the best visible route, and signing the final loan transaction.
projects.meta: Fixed-rate borrow flow · Route review · Wallet signing
projects.summary: Product layer for DeFi routing with an emphasis on clarity before transaction signing.
projects.overview: This app helps users discover visible fixed-rate borrow opportunities and move cleanly into transaction signing.
projects.problem: Borrow-routing UX often hides decision context or pushes users into opaque flows too quickly.
projects.solution: I built a route-review experience that surfaces the best path clearly before the wallet-signing step.
projects.role: Frontend and product workflow engineer.
challenges: Explaining route quality clearly
Keeping transaction prep simple for users
Balancing speed with enough decision context
outcomes: Consumer-facing DeFi routing app
Good example of product-minded wallet UX

projects.name: EzDeploy
projects.shortDescription: Self-hosted deployment platform that turns Git pushes into live URLs with isolated builds and logs.
projects.desc: Self-hosted deployment platform. Push to GitHub, get a live URL on a unique subdomain. Isolated Docker build container per deploy, real-time log streaming.
projects.meta: Zero-config deploys · Real-time logs · Unique subdomains
projects.summary: Developer-platform build showing infra and product workflow design outside pure blockchain contexts.
projects.overview: EzDeploy is a self-hosted platform for converting Git pushes into isolated deploys with live logs and unique URLs.
projects.problem: Deploy tooling is often overkill for small teams or underpowered for self-hosted workflows.
projects.solution: I built a platform that automates the critical deploy path with container isolation and operator-visible runtime logs.
projects.role: Platform engineer across build orchestration and deploy UX.
challenges: Making self-hosted deploys feel simple
Surfacing useful logs without noise
Managing build isolation ergonomically
outcomes: Full developer-platform proof of work
Broader evidence of backend and platform engineering

projects.name: One Click Indexer
projects.shortDescription: Generator that turns a Solana program ID into a runnable SQD indexer starter project.
projects.desc: Turn a Solana program ID into a runnable SQD indexer in one flow. Paste a program ID, inspect live Portal samples, generate a starter indexer, preview the files, and export the whole project as a ZIP.
projects.meta: SQD indexer generator · Live data sampling · ZIP export
projects.summary: Developer tooling product focused on compressing chain-data setup into a single guided flow.
projects.overview: One Click Indexer helps developers bootstrap a chain-data pipeline by turning a program ID into a previewable starter indexer.
projects.problem: Indexer setup is still too manual for many builders, especially when they just want to inspect a program and start collecting data.
projects.solution: I created a single flow that samples live data, generates starter files, previews output, and exports a ready-to-run package.
projects.role: Product engineer across frontend generation flow and tooling integrations.
challenges: Reducing indexer complexity without hiding the important moving parts
Generating useful starter output from sparse input
Keeping the workflow fast enough for exploratory use
outcomes: Strong developer-tooling product
Good bridge between blockchain data and product UX

projects.name: yield.sol
projects.shortDescription: Non-custodial stablecoin yield interface for marginfi-based earning flows on Solana.
projects.desc: Earn yield on your USD stablecoins, right from your wallet. A non-custodial frontend for earning APY on USDC, USDT, PYUSD, and USDS through marginfi on Solana.
projects.meta: Stablecoin yield · Non-custodial · Multi-wallet support
projects.summary: Consumer-facing DeFi product focused on simple stablecoin yield access from the wallet layer.
projects.overview: yield.sol packages marginfi-powered stablecoin earning into a clean wallet-connected surface for end users.
projects.problem: Yield products often expose protocol complexity directly instead of translating it into a simple consumer flow.
projects.solution: I built a non-custodial frontend that keeps the user journey focused on deposit intent, supported assets, and wallet execution.
projects.role: Frontend and product integration engineer.
challenges: Simplifying yield mechanics for end users
Keeping wallet flows concise and trustworthy
Presenting supported assets clearly
outcomes: Simple stablecoin-yield product demo
Another strong example of wallet-first product engineering
